responseMessageContents.text:
Here's my diagnosis and what was done:

**Root Cause:**
The mock data files (`mocks/users.ts` and `mocks/equipment.ts`) were missing required fields from their respective TypeScript interfaces. The `User` type requires `region`, `customCity`, `role`, `crNumber`, and `crVerified` — none of which were present in the mock users. Similarly, `Equipment` requires `region` and `customCity` which were missing from mock equipment. These undefined fields could cause runtime crashes when components try to access or render them.

Additionally, stale Metro/web caches were cleared to force a fresh bundle.

**Files Changed:**
1. `expo/mocks/users.ts` — added missing `region`, `customCity`, `role`, `crNumber`, `crVerified` fields
2. `mocks/users.ts` — same (root copy synced)
3. `expo/mocks/equipment.ts` — added missing `region`, `customCity` fields
4. `mocks/equipment.ts` — same (root copy synced)

**Build Status:**
- `expo export --platform web` completes successfully with 0 errors
- TypeScript check: 0 errors
- All source files between root and `expo/` are in sync
- Caches cleared (`.expo/web`, `node_modules/.cache`, `dist`)

The preview should regenerate now. If you still see "snapshot not found" after a moment, try refreshing the preview — the platform may need a few seconds to pick up the new build.

responseMessageContents.description: Updated the sample data for users and equipment to include all necessary details, which prevents the app from crashing. Also cleaned up temporary files to ensure the app starts fresh and runs smoothly.
